opportunities.rationale: Iran strait tensions keep WTI near $98; XOM benefits from higher crude realisations while UAL faces acute jet fuel cost pressure (fuel ~25% of CASM). XOM gapped up 5.6% on 3/22 while UAL gapped down 2.3% — intraday spread widened ~8% in a single session. Goldman Brent target $110 supports continued energy outperformance. Invalidated on verified ceasefire or Brent collapse below $85. Spread stop at -3%.

Second energy-vs-airlines pair using ALK as the weakest US carrier (down 12.7% from prev_close on 3/22, no fuel hedging program, heavy leisure-route exposure). CVX gapped up 6.2% on 3/22 as oil spiked. ALK has the highest unhedged fuel cost exposure among US airlines and the smallest balance sheet to absorb $98 WTI. The CVX/ALK spread moved ~19% in a single session — extreme divergence with strong catalyst continuation. Invalidated on Hormuz de-escalation or OPEC emergency supply release. Spread stop at -3%.

Sector Rotation archetype — energy is the only green GICS sector while SPY sells off in war/stagflation regime. XOM gapped up 5.6% on 3/22 while SPY gapped down 3.5% — single-day spread widened ~9%. This pair captures the macro energy rotation without single-stock short risk. VIX at 26.78 (Extreme Fear) supports continued defensive rotation into energy. Oil above $95 is the key regime condition. Invalidated on ceasefire + oil below $85 or a strong risk-on FOMC pivot. Spread stop at -3%.

Quality vs Reset in the GLP-1 obesity space. LLY dropped 8.3% this week but tirzepatide pipeline and commercial execution are intact (Mounjaro/Zepbound revenue run-rate >$20B). NVO continues to reprice lower on 2026 guidance cuts, Cagrisema Phase 3 weight-loss miss (only 22.7% vs LLY's 26.6%), and EU pricing pressure. LLY/NVO price ratio at 24.8x vs 6-month average of ~22x — but LLY is falling faster this week (-8% vs NVO -3.8%), creating a short-term entry discount. MODERATE because LLY is being dragged down by broad market risk-off — the catalyst is intact but timing is uncertain. Invalidated if LLY breaks below $878 or NVO announces positive Cagrisema update. Spread stop at -3%.

Defensive Quality vs Reset in consumer staples. COST's membership model is recession-resilient with higher-income demographics and 93% renewal rates; WMT faces tariff-driven supply chain pressure, margin compression from e-commerce investment, and a stretched 30x forward P/E. Both declining but COST down 3.6% vs WMT down 5.9% from 3/13 — COST already outperforming on a relative basis, with the gap widening as risk-off deepens. On 3/22: COST -2.6% vs WMT -3.9% from prev_close. Invalidated if WMT announces tariff mitigation plan or COST membership growth decelerates. Spread stop at -3%.

opportunities.conflict_note: CCL is LONG in pre-earnings.json (1-day hold, exit before Mar 27 BMO). Do NOT initiate the CCL short leg until after CCL earnings print on Mar 27 BMO to avoid being simultaneously long and short CCL.
opportunities.rationale: Defensive vs Cyclical archetype — defense contractor vs cruise line in a war regime. LMT is the primary PAC-3 MSE and F-35 beneficiary ($9.8B contract, DoD budget expansion imminent); CCL faces rising bunker fuel costs ($98 WTI), Middle East itinerary cancellations, and consumer discretionary pullback in Extreme Fear. LMT pulled back 6.6% on 3/22 in a broad selloff, offering a better entry than last week's $645-671 range — but this pullback makes timing uncertain (MODERATE, not HIGH). CCL also dropped 6.5% on 3/22, confirming cruise weakness. Invalidated on ceasefire + normalised oil + consumer confidence rebound. Spread stop at -3%.
